message:
fix(security): close TOCTOU window in generate_signing_key + correct git-dir + index-blob handling in pre-commit hook

Fresh-eyes review of the security-prevention code committed in 1b5ebae
turned up two real bugs and one defensive-code gap:

(1) scripts/share_to_github_pages.py: generate_signing_key() did
    write_bytes() then chmod(0o600). Two issues with that order:

    a. There was a brief window where the file existed with the
       process's default umask (commonly 0644 — world-readable). If
       any other process scanned ~/.mcp-agent-mail/signing-keys
       during that window, it could read the seed before chmod
       restricted access. Window is short but real.

    b. write_bytes() will silently overwrite an existing file. With
       only 32 bits of randomness in the filename suffix
       (secrets.token_hex(4)), a hex collision against a previously
       generated key would silently destroy the prior seed. Anyone
       who had signed bundles with the old key would no longer be
       able to update them.

    Fix: open the file via os.open(path, O_WRONLY|O_CREAT|O_EXCL,
    0o600). O_EXCL fails if the path exists (collision becomes a
    loud error instead of a silent overwrite). Passing the mode to
    os.open sets the file mode at creation time before any data is
    written, eliminating the umask window. Wrap the fd in
    os.fdopen() for the write so Python's file-object machinery
    handles the close. On collision, retry up to 8 times (each retry
    re-rolls the hex via secrets.token_hex(4)) before raising — 8
    attempts × 2^32 hex space makes spurious failure essentially
    impossible. If the write itself fails after the file was
    created, unlink it so we don't leave a zero-byte orphan that
    future collision-checks would trip over. Keep the post-write
    chmod as defense in depth (some network filesystems treat the
    O_CREAT mode as advisory).

    Verified locally: smoke test confirms the resulting file has
    mode 0o600 and exactly 32 bytes after generation, and the path
    lives under ~/.mcp-agent-mail/signing-keys/ as intended.

(2) .githooks/pre-commit: two related correctness bugs.

    a. The merge/rebase short-circuit was `[[ -f .git/MERGE_HEAD
       || -d .git/rebase-merge || -d .git/rebase-apply ]]` — assumes
       .git is a directory under the repo root. In a `git worktree`,
       .git is a *file* containing "gitdir: <path>", and
       MERGE_HEAD/rebase-* live under that resolved gitdir
       (typically under the main repo's .git/worktrees/<name>/).
       The literal path checks would always fail in worktrees,
       causing the hook to scan during merge commits when it should
       skip. Fix: resolve via `git rev-parse --git-dir` (handles
       both forms) and use $GIT_DIR for all four path checks.

    b. The size gate read the *working tree* file size
       (`wc -c <"$path"`) but the actual scan reads the *indexed
       blob* (`git show ":$path"`). After `git add` the two can
       diverge — a working-tree file edited after `git add` would
       have a different size than what's about to be committed.
       Worse, if the file isn't present in the working tree at all
       (e.g. staged from a different branch via cherry-pick), the
       `wc -c` would error and `set -e` would abort the hook
       entirely. Fix: use `git cat-file -s ":$path"` for the size
       check too, with `|| echo 0` so an unreadable index entry
       falls through to "size 0, skip" rather than aborting.

(3) Cosmetic: explicit comments on both fixes documenting why the
    safer-but-uglier API was chosen, so a future cleanup pass
    doesn't "simplify" them back.

Verified the updated hook with a fake `test-fake-2.key`: hook exits
1 with the expected "matches private-key filename pattern"
violation, both inside a normal checkout and resolving GIT_DIR
correctly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -14,8 +14,16 @@ set -euo pipefail
 red()  { printf '\033[31m%s\033[0m\n' "$*" >&2; }
 yel()  { printf '\033[33m%s\033[0m\n' "$*" >&2; }
 
-# Skip merge commits and rebases
-if [[ -f .git/MERGE_HEAD || -d .git/rebase-merge || -d .git/rebase-apply ]]; then
+# Resolve the actual gitdir. In a regular checkout this is `<repo>/.git`,
+# but in a `git worktree`, `.git` is a *file* containing
+# "gitdir: <path>" — a literal `[[ -f .git/MERGE_HEAD ]]` would always
+# fail there because `.git/MERGE_HEAD` doesn't exist as a path under a
+# file. `git rev-parse --git-dir` resolves both forms.
+GIT_DIR=$(git rev-parse --git-dir 2>/dev/null || echo .git)
+
+# Skip merge commits and rebases — these don't represent "did the user
+# mean to add this" decisions; the index is being assembled by git itself.
+if [[ -f "$GIT_DIR/MERGE_HEAD" || -d "$GIT_DIR/rebase-merge" || -d "$GIT_DIR/rebase-apply" ]]; then
     exit 0
 fi
 
@@ -62,10 +70,12 @@ done
 
 # 2. Content-based heuristics on small staged files (<=64 KB).
 #    Catches tokens that don't have suspicious filenames.
+#    Both the size check and the blob read use the *index* state so we're
+#    inspecting exactly what would be committed, not whatever the working
+#    tree happens to contain (those can diverge after `git add`).
 for path in "${staged[@]}"; do
-    [[ -f "$path" ]] || continue
-    size=$(wc -c <"$path")
-    [[ "$size" -le 65536 ]] || continue
+    size=$(git cat-file -s ":$path" 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
+    [[ "$size" -gt 0 && "$size" -le 65536 ]] || continue
 
     blob=$(git show ":$path" 2>/dev/null || true)
     [[ -n "$blob" ]] || continue

@@ -16,6 +16,7 @@
 from __future__ import annotations
 
 import json
+import os
 import re
 import secrets
 import shlex
@@ -609,20 +610,59 @@ def generate_signing_key() -> Path:
     history until git-filter-repo cleanup. The fix: always write to
     ``~/.mcp-agent-mail/signing-keys/`` (a directory that is, by
     construction, not inside any git repo).
+
+    The file is created atomically with O_CREAT|O_EXCL and mode 0o600 so
+    there is no window where it exists with the default umask (potentially
+    world-readable) and a hex collision against an existing key fails
+    loudly instead of silently overwriting it.
     """
     safe_dir = _safe_signing_key_dir()
-    key_path = safe_dir / f"signing-{secrets.token_hex(4)}.key"
-    key_path.write_bytes(secrets.token_bytes(32))
-    # Set secure permissions (best-effort on Windows where this may not apply)
-    with suppress(OSError, NotImplementedError):
-        key_path.chmod(0o600)
-    console.print(f"[yellow]⚠ Private signing key saved to:[/] {key_path}")
-    console.print(
-        "[yellow]⚠ Back up this file securely - you'll need it to update the bundle.[/]\n"
-        "[dim]Note: keys are written to ~/.mcp-agent-mail/signing-keys/ (never the cwd) "
-        "to keep them out of any git repository.[/]"
-    )
-    return key_path
+
+    # Try a few times in the unlikely event of a hex collision.
+    last_error: OSError | None = None
+    for _ in range(8):
+        candidate = safe_dir / f"signing-{secrets.token_hex(4)}.key"
+        try:
+            # O_CREAT|O_EXCL: refuse to open if path exists. mode=0o600 sets
+            # the file mode at creation time before any data is written, so
+            # there is no TOCTOU window where another process could read it
+            # at the default umask.
+            fd = os.open(
+                candidate,
+                os.O_WRONLY | os.O_CREAT | os.O_EXCL,
+                0o600,
+            )
+        except FileExistsError as exc:
+            last_error = exc
+            continue
+        try:
+            with os.fdopen(fd, "wb") as fh:
+                fh.write(secrets.token_bytes(32))
+        except BaseException:
+            # The fd was opened but writing failed; remove the empty file
+            # so we don't leave an orphan zero-byte key behind that future
+            # collision-checks would trip over.
+            with suppress(OSError):
+                candidate.unlink()
+            raise
+        # Belt-and-suspenders: re-chmod in case the umask interacted oddly
+        # on a platform where mode= in os.open is advisory (notably some
+        # network filesystems). Best-effort on Windows.
+        with suppress(OSError, NotImplementedError):
+            candidate.chmod(0o600)
+        console.print(f"[yellow]⚠ Private signing key saved to:[/] {candidate}")
+        console.print(
+            "[yellow]⚠ Back up this file securely - you'll need it to update the bundle.[/]\n"
+            "[dim]Note: keys are written to ~/.mcp-agent-mail/signing-keys/ (never the cwd) "
+            "to keep them out of any git repository.[/]"
+        )
+        return candidate
+
+    raise RuntimeError(
+        "Failed to allocate a unique signing key filename after 8 attempts. "
+        "This is astronomically unlikely under secrets.token_hex(4); inspect "
+        f"{safe_dir} for stale files or filesystem corruption."
+    ) from last_error
 
 
 def export_bundle(
